title: Spherified Brazilian Red Potatoes Paella
description: A romantic, peppery and crispy paella dish from Brazil featuring spherified red potatoes, ready for a Buddhist temple-friendly dinner. This recipe is a modern twist on a classic dish, perfect for a weeknight dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add 1 minced garlic and 1 chopped onion to the pan and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add 2 cups of diced tomato and 1 cup of chopped olives to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of smoked paprika to the pan and bring to a boil.
Add 2 pounds of red potatoes, sliced, to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes or until they start to soften.
Using a slotted spoon, remove about half of the potatoes from the pan and set aside.
Add 1 tablespoon of saffron threads and 1 teaspoon of red pepper flakes to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the reserved potatoes back into the pan and stir to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix together 2 tablespoons of aquafaba and 1 teaspoon of gelatin to make a spherification liquid.
Using a syringe or spoon, drop small amounts of the spherification liquid onto the surface of the paella and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the spheres have formed.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with additional saffron threads and red pepper flakes if desired.
